kept.text:
A dark-money campaign tied to OpenAI, Palantir Technologies and Andreessen Horowitz is secretly paying influencers to push pro-U.S. AI and anti-China narratives on TikTok/IG.

Influencers are reportedly offered up to $5K per post, given scripts like “China beating the US in AI could threaten our kids’ data,” and told not to disclose or tag the campaign (Build American AI).

Source: Wired

profile_bio.description: Breaking news, reports, and opinions from ongoing clashes of the world.

kept.text:
تسريب خطير يكشف حملة أموال مظلمة تعمل في الخفاء لشراء المحتوى وصناعة رأي عام مزيف بشأن الذكاء الاصطناعي، باشراف OpenAI و Palantir Technologies و Andreessen Horowitz.

الحملة تموّل مؤثرين على منصات الفيديو القصير وتدفع لهم مبالغ تصل إلى 5 آلاف دولار مقابل المنشور الواحد — بشرط واحد:
الترويج لروايات مؤيدة لأمريكا ومعادية للصين دون الكشف عن الجهة التي تقف وراء ذلك.
المؤثرون يحصلون على نصوص جاهزة للترويج للخوف مثل:
“تفوق الصين في الذكاء الاصطناعي قد يهدد بيانات أطفالنا.”

ويُطلب منهم صراحة عدم وضع وسم إعلان، وعدم الإشارة إلى المشروع المعروف باسم Build American AI، ليبدو المحتوى طبيعيًا وعفويًا… بينما هو في الحقيقة ممول وموجّه بالكامل.

كالعادة…
تلاعب بالوعي ، وصناعة روايات سياسية تحت غطاء المحتوى اليومي.
دفع المال للمؤثرين ليتحوّلوا إلى أذرع بروباغندا رقمية دون علم الجمهور هو مستوى جديد من التلاعب الإعلامي.

kept.text:
$PLTR Partner @encompasshlth Encompass Health Corporation (NYSE:EHC) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript 👀⬇️

May 2, 2026
Encompass Health Corporation (NYSE:EHC) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript May 1, 2026...

Doug Coltharp, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer: 
And we are actually developing an enhanced market analysis tool that includes all aspects of the market, including projected growth and real estate aspects in conjunction with Palantir that’s going to help us be a lot more prescriptive when we’re entering in a new market about what should be the initial size and location of the de novo that’s going to anchor that market and then what would be potential sites prioritized in terms of timing for augmenting that with small format hospitals. So that’s going to allow us to be much more proactive with regard to our real estate strategy. And hopefully, even as occupancy ramps up quickly with new capacity coming on board, it’s going to alleviate some of these concerns we have when we’ve fallen behind with regard to bed expansions. 
@PalantirTech 

Operator: We’ll take our next question from Brian Tanquilut with Jefferies.

Brian Tanquilut: Maybe, Doug, since you mentioned Palantir. So when I look at what your hospital — acute care hospitals are doing, rolling out a lot of AI here. Just curious, I mean, what are the initiatives that you’re doing right now? And then when I think of where something like a Palantir can come into place, I look at your length of stay of 12 days right now. Is there room for AI or analytics to improve on the clinical and operational side beyond just kind of like back office stuff?

Douglas Coltharp: Yes, I think there’s a lot going on. Pat, do you want to speak to maybe some of the clinical and operational aspects of it?

Patrick Tuer, Chief Operating Officer:
Yes. The first thing I’ll say in regards to Palantir on the length of stay front, we’re not actively trying to get our length of stay down further. What we’re trying to do is make sure that each patient has an appropriate care plan for their needs. That just happens to average around 12 days. And again, we’re not trying to pressure our hospitals to get that down. So we’re really focused on appropriate patient care for each patient. But we are on the same note, looking for opportunities to make sure that our care plans are as efficient as possible. We’ve talked about our partnership with Palantir on prescreen narratives, appeal letters. But we are looking at and have — are in various stages of implementation of documentation efficiency for our physicians and providers around histories and physicals, face-to-face notes, discharge summaries, and we’re evaluating other opportunities as well.

And this is all in an effort to give our physicians and our clinical staff time back so that they can do direct patient care instead of documenting. And I think there’s going to be operational benefits that come along with that, and we’re excited to see where that goes. We’re really encouraged by some of the things we’re seeing and how efficient it is allowing our providers to be. 🎯

Douglas Coltharp: I would further say just kind of in summary, in the queue with Palantir, we have the real estate analysis and the market analysis project I mentioned before. We’ve got a substantial CRM opportunity. We’ve got a revenue cycle management opportunity, and we’ve got a clinical staffing opportunity as well. Those are all in the queue... 🐂

Encompass Health (NYSE: EHC) is the largest owner and operator of inpatient rehabilitation hospitals in the United States. With a national footprint that includes 167 hospitals in 38 states and Puerto Rico, the Company provides high-quality, compassionate rehabilitative care for patients recovering from a major injury or illness, using advanced technology and innovative treatments to maximize recovery. Encompass Health is ranked as one of Fortune's World's Most Admired Companies™, Becker's Hospital Review's 150 Top Places to Work in Healthcare and Forbes' Most Trusted Companies in America. ✔️

quoted_tweet.text:
Good morning ☕️🦀🇺🇸 Happy Saturday!

The stock market had a strong week overall for the period ending Friday, May 2, 2026 (trading days April 28–May 1), with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting weekly gains and new record closes, while the Dow was slightly negative.

Major Indices Performance (Week of April 28–May 1)
•S&P 500: Closed at 7,230.12 (+0.29% on Friday; weekly gain ~0.3–0.6% range across reports). It hit new all-time highs multiple times.
•Nasdaq Composite: Closed at 25,114.44 (+0.89% on Friday; stronger weekly gain). Also set new records, driven by tech.
•Dow Jones Industrial Average: Closed at 49,499.27 (-0.31% on Friday; slight weekly loss).

April as a whole was exceptional: The S&P 500 gained over 9–10% (best monthly performance since 2020), Nasdaq ~15% (best since 2020), and Dow ~7%. This reflected a sharp recovery from earlier geopolitical pressures.

Key Drivers and Themes
•Strong Corporate Earnings: A heavy earnings week boosted sentiment. Big Tech results were mixed but generally supportive:
◦Apple (AAPL) reported strong Q2 results on April 30: Revenue $111.2B (+17% YoY), EPS $2.01 (+22% YoY), beating estimates. Shares rose >3% on Friday.
◦Other highlights included Alphabet (strong results), Caterpillar (big Dow boost), and mixed Big Tech reactions around AI capex/spending concerns (e.g., Meta, Microsoft).
•Geopolitics and Oil: Ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruptions kept oil volatile and elevated (WTI near $100+/barrel at points, with spikes higher). This pressured some sectors but markets largely shrugged it off amid ceasefire hopes and resilient earnings.
•Breadth and Style: Tech and growth led (especially AI-related), while broader market participation was narrower. Value and small-caps were more mixed.
•Economic Backdrop: Resilient U.S. data (e.g., retail sales, PMIs) supported the rally despite Fed signals and inflation concerns. Markets showed optimism heading into May.

Sector and Market Notes
•Tech, communication services, and select cyclicals (e.g., industrials) outperformed.
•Energy was volatile with oil prices.
•Defensive areas lagged somewhat.
•International markets were mixed, with some developed markets underperforming U.S. indices.

Overall Sentiment: Bullish recovery mode. The market demonstrated resilience amid geopolitical noise, rewarding earnings strength and AI optimism. “Sell in May” concerns were discussed but not dominating, given recent momentum.

This recap is based on closing levels and reports as of May 1–2, 2026. Markets move fast—always check real-time sources for the latest.
